story.iWant: spatial rotation drills that challenge my ability to mentally rotate and mirror 2D shapes
story.soThat: I improve my spatial awareness and visual-spatial reasoning through targeted practice
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create SVG Shape Library **Subtask 1.1:** Create `src/features/training/content/shapes.ts` - Export 8-10 pre-defined SVG shape components as React components - Shapes: square, circle, triangle, rectangle, pentagon, hexagon, L-shape, T-shape, arrow, star - Each shape: 120x120px viewBox, centered, simple paths - Use consistent stroke width (2px) and fill (currentColor for theming)
**Subtask 1.2:** Create shape transformation utilities - `rotateShape(degrees: number)`: Apply CSS transform for rotation - `mirrorShape()`: Apply CSS transform for horizontal flip (scaleX(-1)) - Ensure transforms use GPU-accelerated properties (transform, not left/top)
### Task 2: Implement SpatialRotationDrill Component **Subtask 2.1:** Create `src/features/training/drills/SpatialRotationDrill.tsx` - Implement DrillProps interface: `{ difficulty, sessionId, onComplete }` - Component state: referenceShape, comparisonShape, rotationDegrees, isMirrored, correctAnswer, userAnswer, showFeedback, timeToAnswer
**Subtask 2.2:** Implement drill problem generation logic - `generateDrillProblem(difficulty)` function - Selects random shape from library - Applies rotation/mirroring based on difficulty - Returns: { referenceShape, comparisonShape, rotationDegrees, isMirrored, correctAnswer }
**Subtask 2.3:** Implement UI layout - Two-column grid: reference shape (left) | comparison shape (right) - Question prompt: "Are these the same shape?" - Two answer buttons with Framer Motion hover effects - Feedback overlay (green/red flash with checkmark/X icon)
**Subtask 2.4:** Implement interaction handlers - `handleAnswer(userSelection: boolean)` function - Records timeToAnswer, determines isCorrect, shows feedback UI - Creates DrillResult object, calls onComplete(result)
**Subtask 2.5:** Implement accessibility features - Keyboard navigation (Tab, Space, Enter) - ARIA labels and roles - Screen reader announcements for feedback
### Task 3: Extend DrillResult Schema (if not already done) **Subtask 3.1:** Update `src/types/schemas.ts` - Add optional fields: shapeType, rotationDegrees, isMirrored - Verify Dexie schema supports these fields - NOTE: May already be complete from Story 3.2
### Task 4: Integrate SpatialRotationDrill into TrainingSession **Subtask 4.1:** Update `src/features/training/components/TrainingSession.tsx` - Import SpatialRotationDrill component - Add conditional rendering for 'spatial_rotation' drill type - Follow same pattern as NumberLineDrill with React key prop
**Subtask 4.2:** Test auto-skip logic for unimplemented drill types
### Task 5: Write Unit Tests **Subtask 5.1:** Create `src/features/training/drills/SpatialRotationDrill.test.tsx` - Test component renders with shapes - Test answer buttons trigger onComplete - Test feedback displays correctly - Test DrillResult contains required fields
**Subtask 5.2:** Create `src/features/training/content/shapes.test.ts` - Test all shapes render - Test transformations apply correct CSS
### Task 6: Write E2E Test **Subtask 6.1:** Update `tests/e2e/training-drill-flow.spec.ts` - Add test case for Spatial Rotation Drill flow - Follow same pattern as NumberLineDrill E2E test
### Task 7: Manual Testing and Bug Fixes **Subtask 7.1:** Run manual verification steps **Subtask 7.2:** Fix any bugs discovered

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Given** a training session is active with Spatial Rotation drill selected **When** the SpatialRotationDrill component renders **Then** the drill displays: - Two SVG shapes rendered side-by-side (left: reference, right: comparison) - Shapes from pre-defined SVG library (8-10 shapes) - Comparison shape may be: same, rotated (45°, 90°, 180°, 270°), mirrored, or both - Question prompt: "Are these the same shape?" - Two large tap-friendly buttons: "Yes, Same" (green) and "No, Different" (red) - Timer tracking elapsed time (hidden from UI)
**And** Interaction flow: 1. User views shapes and mentally rotates/mirrors 2. User taps answer button 3. Visual feedback: Correct (green flash, success sound, "+1" animation) or Incorrect (shows correct answer with explanation) 4. Auto-advances to next drill after 1.5 seconds
**And** Drill result recorded with module='spatial_rotation', shapeType, rotationDegrees, isMirrored, userAnswer, correctAnswer, isCorrect, accuracy (100 or 0), timeToAnswer, difficulty
**And** Difficulty progression: - Easy: No mirroring, only 90°/180° rotations, simple shapes - Medium: Mirroring OR rotation, 45° angles, complex shapes - Hard: Both rotation AND mirroring, all angles, complex asymmetric shapes
**And** Accessibility: Keyboard support (Tab, Space, Enter), 44px tap targets, ARIA labels, screen reader support, high contrast mode

docs.doc: SpatialRotationDrill component specification (line 126): Mental rotation/mirroring of 2D shapes with DrillProps interface. DrillResult model extensions (lines 178-182): shapeType, rotationDegrees, isMirrored fields for spatial rotation data capture. Acceptance criteria AC-2.2 (line 729): Component test requirements - shapes render correctly, rotation/mirroring applied, answer validated. Manual verification steps (line 758): Start session, advance to drill, verify SVG shapes, select answer, verify feedback, check Dexie persistence. Performance NFR (lines 440-443): SVG shapes must render without layout shift, CSS transforms preferred, no lazy loading during drill.

Project structure (lines 145-199): Feature modules organized as `src/features/training/drills/`, shared components in `src/shared/components/`. Testing philosophy (lines 376-410): E2E tests as final guarantee, manual verification required in running dev server (`npm run dev`), incremental per-story verification. Technology stack (lines 98-140): React 19.2, TypeScript 5.9, Framer Motion 12.23.24, shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, Dexie.js 4.2.1. Naming conventions (lines 304-323): Components PascalCase.tsx, camelCase for functions/variables, SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for constants. Accessibility mandate (lines 412-417): WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, respect prefers-reduced-motion.

DrillInteractionWidget abstract component (lines 956-998): Base patterns for drill UI - states (default, input active, submitted, correct, incorrect), accessibility (keyboard nav, screen reader support). Color system for feedback (lines 232-245): Success green (#66BB6A) for correct, warning gold (#FBD786) for incorrect (not harsh red), celebratory animations. Button hierarchy (lines 1059-1088): Primary coral (#E87461) for submit actions, 48px height touch targets, secondary mint (#A8E6CF) for alternatives. Feedback patterns (lines 1092-1119): Success checkmark + border flash, supportive error messages ("Let's try another approach"), 3s toast auto-dismiss. Spatial rotation drill requirements (line 968-973): Canvas/SVG object, rotation controls (swipe/buttons), angle indicator, reset button.

artifact.reason: Interface contract that SpatialRotationDrill must implement (difficulty, sessionId, onComplete). Story 3.2 established this pattern.
Reference implementation showing drill component structure, feedback UI, Dexie persistence pattern, auto-advance timing (1.5s), and React Hooks best practices.
Extended in Story 3.2 to include shapeType, rotationDegrees, isMirrored fields (lines 58-61). SpatialRotationDrill will populate these fields.
Integration pattern for rendering drills. Must add conditional for 'spatial_rotation' drill type with key prop pattern: key={`drill-${drillIndex}`} to prevent state bugs.
Dexie database setup with drill_results table indexed by sessionId and module. SpatialRotationDrill persists results here with localStorage fallback.
E2E test pattern from Story 3.2. Must extend with spatial rotation drill test case following same structure.

dependencies.framework: Core UI framework with hooks for component state and lifecycle
Type safety for interfaces (DrillProps, DrillResult) and component props
dependencies.library: Animations for feedback transitions, button hover effects, confetti celebrations
IndexedDB wrapper for persisting drill results to drill_results table
UI components - Button (answer buttons), Card (drill container), Toast (feedback notifications)
Utility-first CSS for responsive layout, color system (coral/mint/yellow), spacing scale (8px base)
Icon library for checkmark/X feedback icons
dependencies.test-library: Unit test framework with React Testing Library integration
Component testing with user-centric queries and accessibility focus
E2E testing framework for browser automation and visual testing
Mock IndexedDB for unit tests without real database

constraints.constraint: MUST implement DrillProps interface exactly: { difficulty: 'easy'|'medium'|'hard', sessionId: number, onComplete: (result: DrillResult) => void }. Defined in NumberLineDrill.tsx:18-23.

DrillResult object MUST include: sessionId, timestamp (ISO 8601), module ('spatial_rotation'), shapeType, rotationDegrees, isMirrored, isCorrect, accuracy (100 or 0 binary), timeToAnswer (ms), difficulty. Schema at schemas.ts:43-66.
WCAG 2.1 AA compliance mandatory. Keyboard navigation (Tab, Space, Enter), ARIA labels (role="application", aria-label), 44px minimum tap targets, screen reader support, high contrast mode.

SVG shapes must render without layout shift (fixed dimensions). CSS transforms for rotation/mirroring (GPU-accelerated). Target <16ms render time (60 FPS). No lazy loading during drill (pre-load shapes).
Persist to Dexie drill_results table. Fallback to localStorage on error. Indexed by sessionId and module for fast queries.
Auto-advance after 1.5s feedback delay (consistent with NumberLineDrill). Immediate visual feedback on answer submission. No skippable UI during feedback.
Use key prop in TrainingSession integration: key={`drill-${drillIndex}`} to force new component instances. Prevents state persistence bug from Story 3.2.
Components: PascalCase.tsx. Functions: camelCase. Constants: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. Database columns: snake_case.
Feature-based organization: src/features/training/drills/SpatialRotationDrill.tsx. SVG library: src/features/training/content/shapes.ts. Tests: co-located .test.tsx files.

interface.name: DrillProps
interface.usage: SpatialRotationDrill component MUST implement this interface as component props

interface.name: DrillResult
interface.usage: SpatialRotationDrill creates this object in onComplete callback. Spatial rotation fields MUST be populated.

interface.name: SVG Shape Components
interface.signature: React.FC components for each shape (square, circle, triangle, rectangle, pentagon, hexagon, L-shape, T-shape, arrow, star). Each component: 120x120px viewBox, centered, currentColor fill. Transformation utilities: rotateShape(degrees), mirrorShape() using CSS transforms.
interface.usage: SpatialRotationDrill imports shape components and applies transformations for drill generation

tests.standards:
Unit Tests: Vitest 3.2.4 + React Testing Library 16.3.0. AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert). Co-located .test.tsx files. Mock Framer Motion animations. Use fake-indexeddb for Dexie mocks.
Component Tests: User-centric queries (getByRole, getByLabelText). Accessibility validation. Test states: default, input active, submitted, correct, incorrect. Verify DrillResult object structure.
E2E Tests: Playwright 1.56.1 with running dev server. Test complete user journey: start session → navigate to drill → interact → verify feedback → check persistence. Cross-browser (Chromium primary). Must pass on first run (indicates proper integration).
Coverage Target: 100% per architecture.md. All acceptance criteria must have test evidence. Manual verification in dev server (`npm run dev`) required per Definition of Done.
Test Philosophy: E2E as final guarantee, not primary integration check. Each story verifies own integration points before marking done.
tests.locations:
Unit Tests: src/features/training/drills/SpatialRotationDrill.test.tsx (component tests) src/features/training/content/shapes.test.ts (SVG library tests)
E2E Tests: tests/e2e/training-drill-flow.spec.ts (extend existing test suite with spatial rotation case)
Test Commands: npm test # Run all unit/component tests npm run test:watch # Watch mode for development npm run test:e2e # Run Playwright E2E tests npm run test:ui # Vitest UI for debugging
tests.ideas:
**Unit Test Ideas (SpatialRotationDrill.test.tsx):** - Test: Component renders with reference and comparison shapes - Test: Answer buttons ("Yes, Same" / "No, Different") are clickable and trigger onComplete - Test: Feedback displays correctly for correct answer (green flash, checkmark) - Test: Feedback displays correctly for incorrect answer (warning gold, explanation) - Test: DrillResult object contains all required fields (shapeType, rotationDegrees, isMirrored, accuracy, timeToAnswer) - Test: Accessibility - keyboard navigation (Tab to buttons, Space/Enter to select) - Test: Accessibility - ARIA labels present (role="application", aria-label) - Test: Difficulty progression - Easy level has no mirroring, 90/180 rotations only - Test: Difficulty progression - Medium has mirroring OR rotation, includes 45° - Test: Difficulty progression - Hard has both rotation AND mirroring combined - Test: Framer Motion animations properly mocked (no animation errors in tests) - Test: Auto-advance timing - onComplete called after 1.5s feedback delay
**SVG Library Test Ideas (shapes.test.ts):** - Test: All 10 shapes render without errors (square, circle, triangle, etc.) - Test: Shape transformations apply correct CSS (rotateShape applies transform: rotate) - Test: Mirror transformation applies scaleX(-1) - Test: Rotation degrees match expected values (0°, 45°, 90°, 180°, 270°) - Test: Shapes use currentColor for fill (theme compatibility)
**E2E Test Ideas (training-drill-flow.spec.ts):** - Test: Complete assessment prerequisite (creates training plan) - Test: Start training session from Home dashboard - Test: Advance through drills until SpatialRotationDrill renders - Test: Verify two SVG shapes display side-by-side - Test: Click "Yes, Same" or "No, Different" answer button - Test: Verify feedback animation appears (green/gold flash) - Test: Verify auto-advance to next drill after 1.5s - Test: Check Dexie drill_results table has spatial_rotation entry - Test: Verify persisted record includes shapeType, rotationDegrees, isMirrored fields - Test: Mobile viewport responsive behavior (shapes scale correctly)
